title: Mrs. Gupta Speaks
caption: After Harriet Bloom's moving presentation of the community archive, Dev's grandmother rises to speak. Mrs. Gupta, a tiny woman in her late eighties with silver hair and a deep rose sari, addresses the room with quiet dignity. She speaks of living in Willowmere for sixty-two years, of teaching her grandson Dev to plant his first seed in the community garden. Her words are simple but they cut through every prepared argument in the room. The heritage officer dabs her eyes with a tissue. Detective Park clears his throat and looks away. Even Gerald Hooper, the developer who has fought this designation for months, sits with red-rimmed eyes and slumped shoulders. By the time Mrs. Gupta finishes, there is not a dry eye in the room. The silence that follows needs no interpretation.
story_notes: Dev's grandmother speaks about sixty-two years in Willowmere and teaching Dev to plant his first seed. The entire room, including the heritage officer and Gerald Hooper, is moved to tears.
